taskDescription: Click on the key reproductive physiology terms in the text below.
textField:
The *SRY* gene on the Y chromosome directs development of the *testis*. *Sertoli* cells secrete *anti-Mullerian* hormone (AMH), causing regression of the *paramesonephric* ducts. *Leydig* cells produce *testosterone* under *LH* stimulation, which promotes development of the *mesonephric* ducts into male internal genitalia.

The *menstrual* cycle is regulated by the *hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian* axis. During the *follicular* phase, *FSH* stimulates growth of *ovarian* follicles that secrete *oestrogen*. The positive feedback of oestrogen triggers the *LH* *surge*, causing *ovulation* on day 14. The ruptured follicle becomes the *corpus* *luteum*, which secretes *progesterone* to prepare the *endometrium* for implantation.

*Spermatogenesis* occurs in the *seminiferous* tubules and takes approximately 74 days. The *blood-testis* barrier formed by *Sertoli* cells protects developing *spermatocytes* from immune attack. The combined oral *contraceptive* pill prevents pregnancy primarily by suppressing the LH surge and preventing ovulation.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Revisit the terms you missed.
Excellent! You identified the key reproductive physiology terms accurately.
